scenario: Beacon Payments runs Camunda 8.8 and on one order-process instance the operator notices that a previously required variable is missing entirely rather than being typed wrong, blocking 1 specific 480 euro order. The operator wants to use the same Operate flow used for editing variables to seed the missing variable on the instance.
question: How does the documented Operate flow let the operator handle this missing-variable case rather than the wrong-type case?

options.text: In addition to editing an existing variable, Operate lets the operator add a variable when one is missing entirely from a process instance, using the same incident-resolution flow surfaced on the instance view

Operate only supports editing variables that already exist on the process instance, so a missing variable can only be supplied by cancelling the instance and recreating it with a complete variable payload again

Operate exposes a separate Seed Variable wizard reachable only from the Optimize dashboard, where missing variables must be added before any retry control on the Operate process instance can be initiated

Operate auto-generates the missing variable with a null value as soon as the incident is opened, so the operator only needs to retry the failing step and never explicitly adds a variable on the instance

a.text: Correct. The docs note that the operator could solve this particular problem by editing a variable, but a variable can also be added when it is missing from the process instance altogether using the same Operate flow on the instance view.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly state that a variable can be added when it is missing, so the operator does not have to cancel and recreate the instance with a fresh payload just to supply a missing variable.

c.text: Incorrect. Adding a missing variable is part of the Operate incident-resolution flow on the instance view itself, not a separate Seed Variable wizard that lives inside Optimize on the cluster console.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs describe an explicit add-variable action by the operator rather than an automatic null seeding triggered by the incident being opened on the instance.

explanation: Operate supports both editing an existing variable and adding a variable when one is missing from the process instance entirely. Either action is used as part of the documented incident-resolution flow on the affected instance view in Operate.
